title: Edit and add issues from the status-page viewer
description: Extend the status-page viewer from read-only to read-write for issues: add new issues and edit existing ones from the web page. Writes go through the server (EXEC-054/055) since a static page cannot write the .excn JSON; every write must remain schema-valid against issue.schema.json and respect the issue partitioning (backlog vs sprint partitions, scribe ownership of moves). Hard requirements: keep it as lightweight and fast as possible — no framework, minimal surface.
acceptance_criteria: Issues can be added and edited from the status page; writes validate against issue.schema.json and land atomically
Ids are assigned by the server scanning all partitions (scribe-assigns-ids rule honored)
Schema-invalid payloads are rejected with a named error
Partition moves and lifecycle remain agent work
notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Builds on the viewer-server track; interaction with scribe ownership of issue JSON to be resolved at grill time. Re-identified from EXEC-048 on 2026-06-10: the original id collided with a PRD-007 hook slice filed in a parallel session; sprint-4 close gate caught the lineage mismatch.
